goal: The audience should feel the scale and the secrecy of a 101-metre private object — complete, unrepeatable, and never seen inside.

facts.text: The 2025 Moonrise is 101 metres long, built by Feadship to the same design team as the 2020 original
facts.visual_hint: a 101-metre grey-hulled megayacht in full profile

facts.text: Its satellite receivers are integrated into the superstructure instead of the five mast domes the original carried
facts.visual_hint: a clean superstructure with no satellite domes on the mast

facts.text: It has a higher mast, a longer swim platform and an owner's deck
facts.visual_hint: a higher mast and an extended swim platform at the stern

facts.text: The hull is grey with a vertical bow and elongated hull windows, a modern masculine profile
facts.visual_hint: elongated windows running the length of a grey hull above a vertical bow

facts.text: Moonrise has never been chartered and its interior has never been shown publicly
Its support vessel Nebula is 68 metres and carries a full-size hangar for an Airbus H145 helicopter
facts.visual_hint: a 68-metre support vessel with a helicopter hangar

hook.action: The grey hull runs unbroken from a vertical bow, elongated windows tracing its length
hook.ending_frame: The vertical bow stands clean and sheer

escalation.action: The superstructure carries no satellite domes, the receivers sitting flush beneath a higher mast
escalation.ending_frame: The mast rises bare against the sky

payoff.action: The long swim platform opens at the stern as the full hundred-and-one metre profile comes into view
payoff.ending_frame: The 68-metre support vessel sits alongside, its helicopter hangar open

constraints.rule: visible on deck, on the platform or through the windows

hero_moment.description: The uninterrupted silhouette fills the frame, vertical bow, continuous window line and domeless superstructure reading as one seamless hundred-and-one metre profile
